title: Garlicky Gooey Jamaican Yam Hash
description: A sweet and savory Jamaican-inspired dish featuring roasted yams, caramelized onions, and a tangy pickle sauce, perfect for a brunch gathering. This Jain-friendly recipe is free from animal products and by-products.
instructions: Toss the diced yams with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and black pepper on a baking sheet. Roast for 20-25 minutes, or until tender.
In a large skillet, caramelize the onions over medium heat for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and diced scotch bonnet peppers to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Stir in the brown sugar, pickle relish, and roasted yams. Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture is well combined.
Using a spatula, shape the mixture into a hash shape and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the bottom is crispy and golden brown.
Flip the hash and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the other side is also crispy and golden brown.
Serve the Garlicky Gooey Jamaican Yam Hash hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
